description: JSObject::swap Transplant — setIsUsedAsPrototype Triggers GC Outside SuppressGC Region Before Pre-Write Barrier → Shape getObjectClass UAF. CVE-2024-5688 (sec-high). A use-after-free vulnerability exists in SpiderMonkey's object transplant mechanism in js/src/vm/JSObject.cpp. During JSObject::swap, which is used by the object transplant operation, GC suppression is not applied to the entire method. Specifically, JSObject::setIsUsedAsPrototype can trigger a garbage collection, and this call occurs before the pre-write barrier is executed. When GC is triggered at this point, the sweep phase can finalize objects that still have references via shapes, leading to a wild pointer dereference in js::Shape::getObjectClass during JSObject::finalize. The crash manifests when the background sweeping thread tries to read the object class from a shape that points to already-freed memory (0xfffe4b4b00c000c0 poison pattern). The PoC uses transplantableObject(), newGlobal with sameZoneAs, nukeAllCCWs, and transplant in a loop with gczeal(8, 37) to trigger the precise GC timing needed. While the PoC relies on shell-only test functions, the underlying issue exists in code used by the browser for cross-compartment wrapper transplantation. The fix (changeset 09652347e814) extends the existing GC suppression region in JSObject::swap to cover the entire method, including the setIsUsedAsPrototype call.
